title: Audrey Bergner Explores Abandoned Railway Tracks in El Maitén Chubut Under Bright Patagonian Skies
description: El Maitén, Chubut: Audrey Bergner stands confidently amidst the historic steel tracks of the Old Patagonian Express, a testament to Argentina's industrial heritage. The wide-angle lens captures the expansive horizon, emphasizing the convergence of the rustic rail lines that vanish into the distance beneath a brilliant, cloud-dusted azure sky. Flanked by disciplined rows of towering pine trees, the scene evokes a sense of stillness and contemplation inherent to the remote landscapes of the Patagonian steppe. The natural lighting highlights the textured, arid ground where resilient desert flora punctuates the path, contrasting with the refined metal of the dormant train tracks. As she holds her camera equipment, the subject embodies the spirit of the intentional traveler seeking connection with forgotten routes and historical engineering. The interplay of geometric lines and organic growth illustrates the slow reclamation of the environment over the man-made infrastructure of the past. This quiet stretch of track serves as a vital bridge between the historical legacy of steam travel and the contemporary exploration of Argentina’s vast inland territories. Documenting such vestiges of transit infrastructure is essential for preserving the narrative of regional development and the nomadic lifestyle in South America. This image is a collaborative production by Samuel Jeffery and Audrey Bergner for Project 23.
